scope: Local and sanctioned-VM launch-surface audit after the 2026-04-24 paper broker position mismatch and unattributed order recurrence.

remaining_ready_matches.classification: Windows OS feature task, not project launch surface

local_windows_task_scheduler.material_change: The two remaining Stage27 reporting/summary tasks were disabled because they belonged to stale local automation outside the sanctioned VM execution path. Stage27_PaperLive was already disabled.

local_process_audit.note: The only matching local processes during the audit were the inspection commands themselves and gcloud helper processes.

sanctioned_vm_audit.process_check: no active runner process observed beyond the pgrep inspection command

evidence: A broker check after the VM patch detected new QQQ option exposure and a new MSFT buy-to-open order from an unknown source.
Client order identifiers matched multi-ticker style naming, but no active local or VM runner process was found after remediation.
Local stale scheduled tasks and legacy runner tasks have now been disabled.
No recurrence was observed during the post-fencing no-new-order watch.
unattributed_order_source.governance_treatment: Keep this as a pre-arm review gate. It can be cleared only by a fresh broker-flat check, a fresh no-active-runner process check, and a fresh no-new-order watch immediately before arming the exclusive window.

hard_rules_preserved: No exclusive execution window was armed.
No strategy selection or risk policy was changed.
No raw session exhaust or raw trade log was committed.
next_safe_operator_gate: Immediately before any exclusive-window arm, re-run broker flat/open-order check.
Re-run local scheduled task and process launch-surface checks.
Run a short no-new-order watch; if a new broker order appears without an explicit operator launch, stop and investigate instead of arming.
